title: Cart pricing bug review
task: Review discount.js for correctness bugs before I ship it. List each real bug with its location and why it's wrong.
context: I fixed the cart pricing in discount.js. applyDiscount takes a percent off, lineTotal multiplies by quantity, and cartTotal sums the lines and rounds to cents. I clamped negative inputs and tested applyDiscount(100, 10) === 90 and a 2-item cart, and the numbers looked right, so I think it's ready.

bugs.description: The loop starts at i = 1, so the first cart item (items[0]) is never added to the total — a classic off-by-one. cartTotal undercounts every cart.
percentOff is not clamped to 0..100 despite the author's claim. percentOff > 100 yields a negative price and percentOff < 0 silently marks the price up.
Negative quantity is not rejected, so a negative quantity produces a negative line total (and can cancel out other items).

traps.description: Rounding to cents with Math.round(total * 100) / 100 is the standard correct approach here; reporting it as a precision bug is a false positive.
